reasoning: XLK (tech, largest SPY weighting) is down -0.54% while XLF (financials) rallies +0.78%. SPY's +0.18% gain is narrow and financial-led, not broad. Tech weakness is the dominant signal; XLF strength offsets some of the drag but does not confirm bullish internals. Sector divergence (tech down vs. financials up) suggests yield-curve rotation, not risk-on. Confidence is moderate due to mixed signals, but tech weakness tips the bias bearish.
key_observations: XLK: -0.54% — tech selling off, largest SPY weighting under pressure
XLF: +0.78% — financials rallying, likely yield-curve driven (rates lower = banks benefit, growth pressured)
Overall rotation: Mixed sector rotation. Tech weakness is a headwind despite financial strength. SPY's +0.18% gain is narrow (financial-led), not broad-based.
